topics.label: Agents
topics.summary: Heavy discussion around autonomous coding agents, multi-agent orchestration patterns, and the first browser-native agents reaching production reliability.

posts.text: OpenAI Operator mode can now fill forms, book appointments, and navigate any website in the browser. It asks before consequential actions. This is the beginning of the agent layer.
posts.summary: OpenAI Operator ships browser-level agency including form filling, booking, and navigation with consent checkpoints.
posts.whyItMatters: Browser-native agents with consent gates marks the practical start of the agentic automation era.

posts.text: Agentic coding loops: what breaks most often based on 200 hours of running unsupervised agents. The answer is context management, not capability. Here's what I learned.
posts.summary: Shares findings from 200 hours of unsupervised agent runs: context management is the primary failure mode, not model capability.

posts.text: Multi-agent orchestration patterns after building 20+ production systems: fork-join, sequential, and debate patterns each have specific failure modes.
posts.summary: Breaks down multi-agent orchestration patterns from production experience.

topics.label: Frontier Models
topics.summary: GPT-5 hits 1M context, o3-mini launches at lower cost, and Gemini 2.5 Pro posts expert-level chemistry results. The capability race is accelerating.

posts.text: GPT-5 context window is now 1M tokens in the API for all paid tiers. Writing code that touches your entire codebase in one call is now real. Pricing stays the same.
posts.summary: GPT-5 gets a 1M-token context window across all paid API tiers at unchanged pricing.

posts.text: o3-mini is now available in the API. 40% cheaper than o3, same reasoning quality on most coding and math benchmarks. Rate limits are 2x higher at launch.
posts.summary: o3-mini launches in the API at 40% lower cost than o3 with doubled rate limits.

topics.label: Developer Workflows
topics.summary: AI-assisted coding hitting real productivity thresholds: full module rewrites overnight, Figma-to-code pipelines at 65% accuracy, and agentic deployment at 70% first-try reliability.

posts.text: Claude Code just became my primary IDE. Rewrote a 2000-line Swift module overnight and all tests pass.
posts.summary: Reports using Claude Code to fully rewrite a 2000-line Swift module overnight with all tests passing.

posts.text: Built an agent that generates, tests, and deploys microservices end-to-end from a single prompt. Ships working code 70% of the time on first try.
posts.summary: Demonstrates an agent that autonomously generates, tests, and deploys microservices from a single prompt at 70% success rate.

topics.label: LLMs
topics.summary: Research-focused discussion on attention mechanisms, open-model benchmarks, and JEPA's self-supervised video understanding results challenging the language-supervision assumption.

posts.text: nanoGPT v2 is out. Added flash attention, gradient checkpointing, and a cleaner training loop. ~40% faster on A100.
posts.summary: nanoGPT v2 ships with flash attention and 40% training speed improvement on A100.

posts.text: JEPA: new results on self-supervised video understanding. No language supervision required — just patch-level prediction.
posts.summary: New JEPA results show state-of-the-art video understanding via self-supervised patch prediction.

topics.label: Open Source
topics.summary: Open-source models gaining ground: Reflection-Llama beats GPT-4o on reasoning, Qwen2.5-72B climbs leaderboards, and new fine-tuning tutorials democratize access.

posts.text: Reflection-Llama-3.1-70B beats GPT-4o on 6 of 8 reasoning benchmarks. Open-sourcing weights and training recipe today.
posts.summary: Open-sources Reflection-Llama-3.1-70B, which beats GPT-4o on most reasoning benchmarks.

topics.label: Startup Ideas
topics.summary: Solo founders posting MRR milestones with AI leverage. The market is filtering AI wrappers — distribution and data moats are emerging as the real differentiators.

posts.text: Nomad List crossed 500k registered users. No team. AI handles 70% of support, 40% of the feature roadmap.
posts.summary: Nomad List reaches 500k users as a one-person operation with AI handling most support and roadmap decisions.

posts.text: Month 3 update: $23k MRR across 4 AI products, all built solo with AI assistance.
posts.summary: Reports $23k MRR from 4 solo-built AI products after 3 months.

topics.label: Prompting
topics.summary: Practical prompt engineering patterns gaining traction: chain-of-density for summaries, skeptical reviewer framing for reliability, and replacing entire app workflows with single prompts.

posts.text: Chain-of-density prompting: get 5x more information density in AI summaries through iterative compression. Full guide with before/after comparisons.
posts.summary: Publishes a guide on chain-of-density prompting for higher-information summaries with reusable templates.

topics.label: Research Papers
topics.summary: Strong week for applied science: AlphaFold-style materials prediction, JEPA video results, and expert-level chemistry benchmarks. The weekly paper digest covers 14 notable releases.

posts.text: Applying AlphaFold-style structural prediction to materials science. Crystal stability prediction 2x better than DFT at 1/100th the compute.
posts.summary: Google DeepMind adapts AlphaFold's approach to materials science with promising crystal stability results.

topics.label: Monetization
topics.summary: AI creative tools disrupting ad agencies, $140/month stacks generating $400k MRR, and enterprise deployments staying quiet for competitive reasons.

posts.text: My AI tools stack in 2025: Claude for writing and code, Midjourney v7 for images, Suno for marketing audio. Total monthly cost: $140. Revenue: $400k/month.
posts.summary: Shares a $140/month AI tools stack generating $400k/month in revenue.

topics.label: Images / Video
topics.summary: AI image tools entering professional design and advertising workflows: Figma + Claude at 3x handoff speed, AI ad creatives outperforming humans on most accounts.

posts.text: AI + Figma workflow: using Claude to generate component variants, write accessibility annotations, and draft design specs. 3x faster on design handoff.
posts.summary: Demonstrates using Claude inside a Figma workflow at 3x handoff speed.
